name: fluid-thermal
description: Initial pore-pressure and temperature fields are set on gridpoints, not zones — they are nodal scalar quantities. Bulk fluid properties such as density are set via 'zone water'. Keyword sets are binary-validated against FLAC2D 9.0.

notes: Pore-pressure, saturation, and head are nodal scalar quantities → use 'zone gridpoint initialize <kw>' (NOT 'zone initialize <kw>').
Gridpoint pore pressures are used to derive zone pore pressures.
Effective stresses depend on pore pressure when using effective-stress constitutive behavior.
'fluid-density' is a 'zone initialize' keyword (per-zone fluid density).
For BULK water density use 'zone water density <value>' (a 'zone water' subcommand, not 'zone initialize').
Temperature is a nodal quantity → use 'zone gridpoint initialize temperature' (NOT 'zone initialize temperature').
Thermal initialization must be paired with thermal material properties when thermal-mechanical coupling is modeled.
'thermal-timestep' is a 'model thermal' configuration, not an initialize keyword.
'thermal-expansion' is a 'zone property' material property, not an initialize keyword.

common_patterns.name: Initialize phreatic pore pressure then fix drain
commands: zone face apply pore-pressure 0 range group 'drain'
notes: FLAC2D gradient tuple is (gx,gy); FLAC3D is (gx,gy,gz).
